results.title: 😸 Study your way across Japan | Product Hunt
results.content:
newsletter icon

July 18th, 2026

# Study your way across Japan

## 😸 Study your way across Japan

gm legends, happy Saturday.

Today: a solo dev turned Japan into a progress bar, a synth claims it can outlast the universe, and your agent finally gets a real text editor.

## Turn Japan into a progress bar

Michał Paczyński (@xpaczka) got tired of Japanese apps skipping vocabulary for drills and games, so he built Mainichi himself: five-minute spaced-repetition sessions on iOS, 20,000 words, and a map of Japan that opens up one prefecture at a time as you learn. [...] 🔥 Our Take: Language apps measure progress in streaks and JLPT levels, numbers that mean nothing until you've been at it a year. Mainichi measures it in Nagano. The comments picked up on it immediately: unlocking a country beats a green owl guilt-tripping you. It won't out-content Duolingo and it doesn't have to. It just has to be the app you'd rather open on the train.

## This synth outlasts the universe

WX is a synth built around controlled randomness: double-click the radar and it reshuffles the entire engine, oscillators, filter, envelopes, effects, into a new sound you can immediately play. Metehan Sipahi (@metehan\_sipahi) says hearing everything it can generate would take millions of times longer than the age of the universe. [...] 122

Language Learning

Alfabird makes mastering tough alphabets effortless with powerful mnemonics and fun, chat-based lessons. Learn Hangul, Japanese or Cyrillic characters in just a few days—no boring drills, just quick, memorable learning. Ready to crack a new alphabet? Let's go! 🚀

No reviews yetBe the first to leave a review for Alfabird

Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works Everywhere [...] This is language learning reimagined! 🦜📚

The promise of learning a new alphabet in 1-2 days is bold, but the mnemonic-based approach makes it believable. Memory experts have long used mnemonics to achieve seemingly impossible feats, and applying this to alphabet learning is brilliant. It's like having a memory coach specifically for language learning!

The chat-based format is particularly clever:

 No boring drills
 Interactive learning
 Casual, stress-free environment
 Quick progress tracking

Focusing on Hangul and Japanese characters initially is smart - these are alphabets that often intimidate learners despite being quite logical in structure. Breaking down these barriers could open up Korean and Japanese learning to many more people.

Key advantages: [...] Key advantages:

 Rapid learning pace
 Memorable techniques
 Fun, engaging format
 Free options available
 No rote memorization

Perfect for:

 Language enthusiasts
 Business travelers
 Cultural explorers

The chat format meets learners where they're comfortable - on their phones, in a familiar messaging environment. It's like having a knowledgeable friend teaching you through conversation rather than a stern teacher with flashcards.

You're not just teaching alphabets; you're removing one of the biggest barriers to language learning! Keep revolutionizing language education! 🌟🗣️

ElevenAgents by ElevenLabs [...] @franckwebpro Thank you so much for your enthusiastic feedback! We're thrilled to hear you find Fujiyama cool. You're absolutely right - speaking practice is crucial for language learning, just as hands-on coding is essential for programming. We're glad you appreciate this aspect of our app. Regarding your question about adding more languages: As a team deeply passionate about Japanese language and culture, we've made the decision to focus exclusively on Japanese. Our mission is to create the best possible platform for learning Japanese and experiencing Japanese culture. We believe that by concentrating all our efforts on one language, we can offer a truly immersive and comprehensive learning experience. Our goal is not just to teach the language, but to be a gateway to understanding and [...] complete with example - sentences, explanations, and associated images - AI assistance if you're unsure how to respond - Adjustable voice output speed - Practice writing katakana, hiragana, and kanji characters These are the current features of our app. Moving forward, we're eager to explore what additional functionalities and improvements are needed to make this an even better service for Japanese language learners. We'd love to hear your thoughts on how we can further enhance the app to support those studying Japanese.
